title: Aleuritia mallophylla
intro: Aleuritia mallophylla, also known as Aleurites mallophyllus, is a plant species in the family Euphorbiaceae, native to Southeast Asia. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, offering potential health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, which may contribute to its use in traditional medicine for treating various ailments. Aleuritia mallophylla contains bioactive constituents such as flavonoids, alkaloids, and triterpenoids, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects. The plant is often used to make herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and extracts, which are used to treat conditions such as fever, cough, and skin infections.
taxonomy: Aleuritia mallophylla belongs to the family Rubiaceae. This plant is classified within the order Gentianales and the clade Asterids. Further classification places it in the family Rubiaceae, tribe Psychotrieae, and genus Aleuritia. Aleuritia mallophylla is a species within the Rubiaceae family, which also includes coffee and gardenia. It is also known to be a part of the clade Asterids, which consists of flowering plants.
